description:
To properly set the group owner of /var/log/auth.log, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp adm /var/log/auth.log
or 
$ sudo chgrp root /var/log/auth.log
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/auth.log</tt> file contains records information about user
login attempts and authentication processes and should only be accessed by
authorized personnel.
ocil_clause:
/var/log/auth.log does not have a group owner of
adm or
root
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /var/log/auth.log, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/auth.log
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: adm or root
title: Verify Group Who Owns /var/log/auth.log File
